reviews.text: One of the best homestay,fully friendly and also homely behaviour.... uncle and aunty are best...and food fastastic..just want to visit again....!!!
reviews.author: Tumpa Dey

nimmu-house-ladakh.editorialSummary: Serene 1900s mountain hotel with rustic rooms & tents, plus dining, yoga & trekking.

reviews.text: Everything about this place is a 10/10. The rooms are super cute, service is great and the views from the yogashala are insane. The staff is really nice and helpful. Everything is so thoughtfully planned. The food is very heartwarming. Loved the place!
reviews.author: Aparna Gaur

reviews.text: Charming hotel, attentive staff, and excellent food made with produce from the garden. It's a true little corner of paradise. Plan to stay at least 2-3 days to fully enjoy it, especially since there are many places to visit in the surrounding area.
reviews.author: Coralie KERESTEDJIAN

reviews.text:
We stayed here for 2 nights , it is a perfect place , who wants to enjoy nature, silence . Best hospitality by Sonam , Indrani.  Best food . We required Meals without garlic and onion , that was done with perfection.
Nice place and must visit.
reviews.author: Prateek Jain

reviews.text:
A heartfelt haven in the Himalayas ..Nimmu House, Ladakh

We had the most beautiful stay at Nimmu House while acclimatizing for our Ladakh trip, and I can’t recommend it enough. From the moment we arrived, Indrani and Vikram, our gracious hosts, made us feel completely at home.
We stayed in their charming tents..comfortable, beautifully maintained, and designed with such incredible attention to detail. One of the sweetest touches was the handwritten welcome note waiting for us in the tent..it truly set the tone for the warmth and care that followed throughout our stay.
Every part of Nimmu House reflects thoughtful hospitality, from the cozy design of the space to the delicious home-cooked meals. Everything was taken care of so gently and naturally that we could truly relax and just soak in the peace of the place.
Indrani and Vikram went out of their way to ensure we had everything we needed, and it never once felt transactional..it felt personal, like staying with dear friends.
If you’re looking for a place in Ladakh that is soulful, serene, and lovingly run, Nimmu House is it. Thank you, Indrani and Vikram, for such a memorable experience..we’ll carry the feeling of your home with us for a long time to come. 🌸🌺🌸
reviews.author: Monisha Bajaj

reviews.text:
This is a hidden gem you don't want to miss in Ladakh! My stay at Nimmu House was nothing short of amazing. I'm not quite sure why they are listed as a 2 star hotel because they should be listed as a 4 or 5 stars - The location is tucked away in the quiet village of Nimmoo, which is a short 45 mins drive from Leh and you can expect to be fully immersed in nature and stillness. The grounds of the location is filled with apricot and apple trees, (Yes, we were lucky enough to eat these fresh fruits straight from the trees!) sunflowers, and all sorts of different plants. They have a beautiful courtyard where you can sit to read or enjoy their delicious farm to table meals. Everything, and I mean everything, is so fresh and delicious.

It is obvious that there is so much love, attention and care that has been put into making this place one that you'll remember. From the food, to the design of the rooms/tents, to the handwritten welcome note, to the service. Aside from the physical space, what made this experience even more incredible were the staff - A big big thank you Indrani, Vikram, Sonam and the rest of the Nimmu team. Thank you for going over and beyond to make sure that everyone was comfortable and enjoying our stay, and showing us what true hospitality means. And not forgetting the 2 resident pups Zero and Slow ❤️  I'm so grateful to have had this magical experience and will definitely be back!
reviews.author: S W

ahilya-by-the-sea.editorialSummary: Genteel seafront hotel featuring outdoor pools, a spa & gardens, plus a restaurant with a terrace.

reviews.text:
We spent three unforgettable days here for our team offsite, and the experience was nothing short of extraordinary. From the moment we arrived, it felt like home — warm, personal, and yet luxuriously special.

What truly stood out was the hospitality and warmth of the staff. None of them are formally trained in hotel management, yet their intuitive care, attention to detail, and genuine smiles made us feel more welcome than any five-star property ever could. It was hospitality straight from the heart.

The culinary experience crafted by Chef Avinash deserves a special mention — each meal was not just food, but a thoughtfully curated experience, with spreads that delighted us every single time.

And of course, the resort itself — a beautiful blend of antique charm, rustic elegance, natural beauty, and the serene location by the sea — made it the perfect backdrop for our time together.

Ahilya by the Sea is more than a resort — it’s an experience of warmth, beauty, and soulful living. Truly unforgettable. 🌊✨🌿
reviews.author: Minakshi Sharma

reviews.text:
A True Hidden Gem – Pure Bliss at Ahilya by the Sea
We’ve just returned from an unforgettable stay at Ahilya by the Sea. This intimate boutique retreat in Nerul is everything we dreamed of for a peaceful Goa getaway—and far exceeds expectations.
Right from arrival, the staff enveloped us in genuine warmth and exceptional care. Everyone—from the welcoming front desk to housekeeping, and especially the talented resident chef and his team—was incredibly friendly, always with a smile, attentive without being overbearing, and eager to personalise every detail. In a world where hospitality can feel scripted, Ahilya’s authentic, heartfelt service stands out as something truly special.
The location is nothing short of superb—serene, quiet, and tucked away in a peaceful corner of Dolphin Bay (appropriately named!), well removed from the crowds and chaos of Goa’s busier beaches. Surrounded by lush tropical gardens, swaying coconut palms, and the soothing rhythm of waves against the sea wall, it feels like your own private sanctuary. No loud music, no hustle—just pure tranquility, vibrant greenery, and two stunning pools (including that magical infinity-edge one overlooking the sea) where you can truly unwind, read, swim, or simply soak in the views. It’s the ideal spot to disconnect and reconnect with nature and each other.
And the food—oh, the food is outstanding, a real highlight. With a deep focus on fresh, local Goan flavours, the resident chef personally consulted with us, crafting meals tailored to our tastes while showcasing regional gems. Breakfasts were vibrant and nourishing—fresh juices, tropical fruits, perfectly spiced local dishes—while candlelit dinners brought incredible seafood, authentic Goan curries, and inventive yet deeply traditional preparations made with the finest seasonal ingredients. Every dish was bursting with flavour, beautifully presented, and a joyful celebration of Goa’s rich culinary heritage.
If you’re seeking a small, luxurious yet wonderfully understated seaside retreat with top-tier staff, blissful quiet, and some of the best local food in Goa, Ahilya by the Sea is unbeatable - it’s pure magic.
Highly recommended—5 stars fall short! 🌴🌊🍤
reviews.author: Geoff rippington

reviews.text:
We stayed at Ahilya by the sea for a week - the minute we stepped into the property, we felt like this is what we needed. Everything right from the location, service, scenery, rooms was superb. Loved the whole antique artsy vibe of the place.
Chef Avinash was kind enough to accommodate all our requests on days we dined at the property. The food was delicious and made with the best local ingredients.
The staff is hands down one of the best in our experience in terms of service. They genuinely take care of you like they mean it!

Highly recommend staying here - this will be our go to place whenever we’re in Goa!
reviews.author: Nancy S M

reviews.text: Staying at Ahilya by the Sea is like stepping into a serene dream—where time slows, the sea sings softly to the shore, and every corner whispers of thoughtful elegance. The hospitality is not merely service; it is heartfelt, gracious, and deeply personal, like being welcomed into a lovely family. Each moment spent there feels wrapped in quiet luxury, where nature and comfort dance in perfect harmony. It is not just a place to stay—it is an experience to be remembered, cherished, and longed for once you've left. A true five-star haven, in every sense of the word.
reviews.author: Sangeetha Sundar

reviews.text: This is definitely the best hidden gem Goa has to offer. Nested right next to the beach, this place offers the best view. I stayed here for one night and really wished it was longer. The staff is super friendly, and chef Avinash is hands down the best - based on our taste preferences he recommended some amazing food options which truly did not disappoint at all. The rooms are very spacious and overlook the ocean. This property has really preserved their heritage and I can’t wait to go back again soon! If you are visiting Goa, please book this for the guaranteed best time!
reviews.author: Shreya Tewatia

thehraav-by-the-river.displayName: Thehraav By The River - Mudhouse Odyssey

reviews.text:
One of the most beautiful and fulfilling stays in the north. Experience the best of Tirthan Valley and experience immense peace and serenity at heart at Thehraav. We were there with a 4 yo and believe me not just us, he too was in love with the place. Aman and Kamal became his best friends along with the best hosts they already are. The place is located on the river and it's beautiful feeling to experience the sounds of gushing waters. The restaurant space feels like home where you can sit and chill for hours with really delicious food from the kitchen. The staff is so friendly and helpful and engages you as they explain and share stories about the place.
Heart is full and memories are loud and beautiful of this place. It's a great retreat for anyone who wants to experience the hills, for chilling at the room or property, for trekking around, for fishing as a few fellow traveller's were into.
reviews.author: samyak gandhi

reviews.text: Loved our stay at Thehraav! The name is so apt for the location and experience. It actually feels so tranquil, stable and a quite and slow life there. It was amazing to unwind there with my parents and husband. We just had a great time sitting by the river, enjoying bonfire in the evenings. The rooms were so cozy with the electric blankets and tall heater towers, we had gone there near to the Christmas and new year time there and it was decorated for those occasions. The staff was very cordial and loved the homely food. I would go back anyday!
reviews.author: Harman Sohi

reviews.text:
I had an absolutely wonderful experience at Thehraav in Tirthan Valley. From the moment I arrived, everything felt peaceful and welcoming. The owner is an incredibly humble and gracious person, always eager to help and genuinely committed to making every guest’s stay memorable.

The property itself is truly serene—nestled by the riverside, surrounded by nature, and designed with charming mud houses and vintage aesthetics. The cottages are beautiful, spotless, and thoughtfully maintained. Sitting by the river, soaking in the calm and quiet, was an experience in itself.

The staff deserves special mention—they are warm, loving, and ever‑ready to support you, making you feel completely at home. The food was simply delightful: home‑style, nourishing, and full of flavor. I especially loved the local Himachali dishes like siddhu, pahadi rajma, and lingri ki sabzi. The ginger‑lemon‑honey tea was an absolute favorite!

What made the stay even more special was the warmth of the village and its people—so humble, polite, and kind‑hearted. It was lovely to see plum, walnut, and apple trees growing right within the property, adding to the organic charm of the place.

My experience at Thehraav was rare, soulful, deeply peaceful, and truly unforgettable. It’s the kind of place you want to return to again and again. My heartfelt gratitude and blessings to the entire team for creating such a beautiful haven.
reviews.author: Arushi Kapoor

reviews.text:
The property is only good for the river view and easy access to the river, continous flowing water noice, garden to play for kids.
The food you order will take atleast 3 hours to prepare.Half of the items on menu are not available. They are always understaffed so expect a lot of delays in all services.
The owner is also not bothered about wtever complain you give or feedback.
reviews.author: harshada agrawal

reviews.text:
Had a great experience overall. The location is excellent and very convenient, and the food quality was impressive. The ambience and service made the visit even more enjoyable. What I particularly liked is that, despite the great quality and location, the pricing is quite reasonable and not overly expensive.

I truly had a wonderful time and would definitely recommend this place to others looking for a river side resort.
reviews.author: Gaurav Gulati

reviews.text: Ridiculous pricing!! What are they thinking?! There are a lot of better experiences in uttarakhand that come at a fraction of this price!
reviews.author: Karthik BN

reviews.text: Waw

reviews.text: Nice pic

reviews.text: Heavenly!

reviews.text: Amazing 😍
reviews.author: Raju Bisht

sunkiya-homestay.displayName: Saanidhya Farm Homestay by Saanidhya Retreats, Mukteshwar

reviews.text: Balcony view is amazing. Stay was comfortable and peaceful. Just around 200meter stretch to reach stay is bit tricky, steep walk. Everything else was good, specially the view from balcony. We walked in the Village Sunkiya where the stay is...the village people are very nice.
reviews.author: Barkha Arora

reviews.text:
Loved the greenery this property is surrounded by. Completely rejuvenated me in every way. The staff is amazingly warm and made some amazing local dishes.

Highly recommend staying here, Saanidhya retreats has my heart.
reviews.author: A.M Chauhan

reviews.text: This homestay exceeded my expectations. The room was spotless, well-decorated, and very comfortable. The two hosts were genuinely friendly and helpful, offering great local tips and making me feel like part of the family. Highly recommend this place to anyone visiting the area!
reviews.author: Rohit M Baldaniya

reviews.text:
As much as I love Mukhteshwar Valley, I also love Saanidhiya homestays and jungle camps.
Arpit and Sonya are great host and friends. We enjoyed kumaoni local cuisines, waterfalls, ramleela . Trip to kasar Devi and back to peaceful homestay
reviews.author: Varun Chadha

reviews.text: I had such a pleasant experience staying here. The homestay had a warm and inviting atmosphere, and the hosts went out of their way to make my stay enjoyable. Their hospitality and attention to detail made a big difference I truly appreciated it!
reviews.author: Tora

reviews.text:
Had such a lovely stay at this beautiful property - The Doksa, nestled right in the middle of the valley. The whole place has a very warm, peaceful and homely vibe that made me feel comfortable almost instantly. It honestly felt like the perfect little escape from the usual rush of life.

What really stood out was how thoughtfully the property has been made. Even though it’s located in such a quaint and remote town, they’ve managed to provide pretty much every amenity and comfort one could think of. The rooms are cozy, clean and extremely comfortable, making it very easy to just relax and enjoy the surroundings, to just be.

The ambience of the place is truly special, waking up to the valley views, the fresh air and the quiet all around was something I genuinely enjoyed every single day.

And the food deserves a big mention. The service was warm and so welcoming, and the food itself was absolutely delicious. There were so many indulgent and elaborate options to choose from, and everything we tried was genuinely tasty and comforting. You can really tell that a lot of care went into curating the overall experience here.

Would definitely recommend this place to anyone looking for a peaceful getaway with great hospitality, amazing food and a stay that just feels easy, comfortable and memorable.
reviews.author: akanksha sharma

reviews.text:
A Beautifully Designed Stay in the Heart of Spiti

Doksa is one of those rare places where the architecture, atmosphere, and landscape come together beautifully.

The property itself is thoughtfully designed — minimal, warm, and perfectly suited to the raw surroundings of Spiti. The large windows opening into the valley views make the entire experience feel incredibly immersive, and the calmness of the place is something we genuinely loved.

After long mountain drives, arriving here truly felt like arriving at a sanctuary.

The hospitality throughout our stay was warm and sincere, and the overall experience felt personal rather than transactional. The food was also very enjoyable, especially considering the remoteness of the location.

That said, it’s also fair to mention that the property is still relatively new, and there are a few operational aspects that are clearly still being streamlined. Service can occasionally feel slightly unpolished, and there are moments where you can sense the property is still settling into itself.

However, none of that took away from the overall experience for us. In many ways, the sincerity of the team and the uniqueness of the place outweighed the small imperfections.

If you’re visiting Spiti and want a stay that feels both authentic and elevated, Doksa is truly a special place.
reviews.author: YUDHVEER SINGH RATHORE

reviews.text:
A little off-roading from Mane village takes you into the magical world of Doksa — a truly unique stay tucked away in the offbeat interiors of Spiti. The silence, slow life, and raw beauty here completely mesmerise you.

Anjali, the hostess, brings such wonderful energy and ensures every guest feels comfortable and cared for. The experiences and activities thoughtfully introduce you to the unique lifestyle of Spiti. Despite being far from nearby towns, Mohit and the team make sure the food leaves you amazed every single time.

We spent two wonderful days here and would absolutely love to return again.
reviews.author: Jatin Makkar

reviews.text: A rare find in a rare location. If you have the gumption to withstand an arduous drive and high altitude, then The Doksa is THE place to go to. We happened to be there on its opening day. While there were a few minor technical glitches, I’m sure Mohit — ably supported by the beautiful Property Manager Anjali — will resolve these very soon. The architecture is minimalist (Scandinavian), the rooms are super cosy, and the food is delectable. A must visit for anyone who loves naked mountains, extraordinary terrain and beautiful night skies!
reviews.author: Ashish Sood

reviews.text: It was a wonderful experience staying at the Doksa. Special thanks to Anjali and the staff who looked after us for the time that we were there. Stargazing and capturing the Milky way at night was one of the highlights of the trip. Look forward to going back soon.
reviews.author: Siddharth Rattan

lchang-nang-retreat.displayName: Lchang Nang Retreat - The House of Trees - Nubra Valley
lchang-nang-retreat.editorialSummary: Rustic cottages, a spa & mountain scenery in a laid-back eco-resort along a river.

reviews.text:
We had a wonderful two-night stay at Lchang Nang Retreat with our family, and it was truly a memorable experience.

What stood out the most was the personal attention by the chef. He went out of his way to cater to our specific dietary preference of no onion and no garlic, and even personally visited our cottage to discuss and plan our dinner and breakfast. That level of care made us feel genuinely valued.

The retreat itself is a peaceful haven, surrounded by breathtaking mountains and stunning natural scenery — perfect for anyone looking to disconnect and relax. The heated rooms and heated mattresses ensured complete comfort despite the cold, and the bonfire setup added a lovely touch to our evenings.

Overall, we absolutely loved our stay and would highly recommend it for families looking for a serene and thoughtful getaway.
reviews.author: SDE Chaitanya

reviews.text:
The location is great. Rooms are very nicely done ensuring the usage of sustainable materials.
Food spread is good. However, there is scope for improvement in the service and choice of items in the buffet. For eg, first day there was no starters in the buffet dinner.
Also, deserts options should definitely be increased. Both the days they served kheer, sewayein and rice kheer.
Service is slow at times. But overall a good place to detox.
reviews.author: rajendra jain

reviews.text:
An unforgettable stay at Lchang Nang, Nubra Valley

Our stay at Hotel Lchang Nang was truly a memorable one. The hospitality extended by the staff was exceptional and made us feel completely at home despite the chilling weather.

A special mention to Pervez, Deepak, and Jagjeevan for their outstanding support and warmth — they went above and beyond to ensure our stay was comfortable and hassle-free. Their attentiveness and genuine care to every minute detail made a huge difference.

The location of the hotel was an icing on the cake of our trip. Breathtaking views of snow-capped mountains, which we simply stared at. The food was delicious, and the provision of tea early in the morning with one of the best cookies ever is worth a mention. The room warmers ensured we stayed cozy and comfortable throughout.

Overall, everything—from service and food to comfort and views—was perfect. Highly recommend Lchang Nang to anyone visiting Nubra Valley. Would love to stay here again!

Thank you Rigzin and the entire team at Lchang Nang.
reviews.author: Mohit Totlani

reviews.text:
It was very comfortable and luxurious stay in Lchang Nang. It is a resort truly built on sustainabilty. Cottages are built using earth with modern amenities and Ladakhi traditionaly touches.

During my stay it was snowing, sub zero temperatures outside and it was super warm, cosy inside, thats true luxury in the harsh winters of Ladakh.

Food was amazing , made from local organic produce. Staff was very nice and hospitable.
reviews.author: satinder saini

reviews.text:
The location is ideal for a quiet idyllic escape. Being able to see the Majestic snow capped mountains and within a short walk be able to see a picturesque river valley is priceless. One can get to some picturesque locations within a couple of hours.The rooms are great but need some sprucing up.
The service from the ops manger the kitchen and dining room staff and the bell boys as well as the housekeeping was very pleasant, thoughtful and respectful. All in all we had a great 3 days here at the Nubra Valley and the resort had a great role to play.
reviews.author: Tridip Dutta

sukoon-houseboat.editorialSummary: Elegant inn, occupying a wooden houseboat on Lake Dal, offering a restaurant, a lounge & a sundeck.

reviews.text:
At Sukoon house boat, the ambience is unlike anything a traditional hotel can offer. Waking up to the birds chirping and watching the morning mist rise off the surface of the Dal lake. The interior was beautifully designed — warm wooden accents, cozy lighting, and thoughtful décor that perfectly complemented the natural setting of Srinagar.
The service was exceptional from start to finish. The staff were warm, attentive, and genuinely passionate about making every guest feel at home.
We had our evening tea on the deck listening to the local Kashmiri music performed by the local artists was truly amazing!!
If you’re looking for something beyond the ordinary — a stay that blends luxury, nature, and genuine character — look no further. I will absolutely be returning, and I cannot recommend this experience highly enough.
reviews.author: Mahitha Reddy

reviews.text:
Our stay on the Sakoon houseboat in Srinagar was absolutely wonderful. The setting on Dal Lake is serene and beautiful — the name “Sakoon,” meaning peace, truly fits the experience. The rooms were clean, comfortable, and tastefully decorated, offering both traditional Kashmiri charm and modern comfort.

What truly made our stay exceptional, however, was the hospitality of Waseem and Pervaiz. They went above and beyond to make us feel at home — always attentive, kind, and thoughtful. Whether it was arranging meals, guiding us on local experiences, or simply sharing stories about Kashmiri culture, their warmth and professionalism made all the difference.

We left feeling refreshed and deeply touched by the care we received. I highly recommend Sakoon to anyone visiting Srinagar — and be sure to ask for Waseem and Pervaiz, who will make your stay unforgettable.
reviews.author: Nita Sood

reviews.text:
Our stay at Sukoon Houseboat was unforgettable, not just because of its serene beauty, but because of the incredible team that made us feel truly cared for from the moment we arrived.

Faizan, the manager, was outstanding. Kind, attentive, and impeccably well-groomed, he ensured every need of ours was met effortlessly. His warmth and professionalism made us feel at home, and his ability to adapt to even the smallest of our requests added immense comfort to our stay.

Waseem, the F&B head, was extraordinary. He went above and beyond to serve us delicious, high-quality meals throughout our stay. Every dish he prepared carried thought, care, and genuine hospitality. Thanks to him, we were constantly well-fed and looked after—he truly made dining on the houseboat a highlight of the experience.

Yasir, who maintained our rooms, deserves a special mention as well. Every day, he ensured our space was spotless, fresh, and well-stocked. We were always hydrated and comfortable because of his consistent, thoughtful service.

And finally, Rashid, our boatman—what a gem. He ensured every journey to and from the houseboat was smooth, convenient, and timely. With him, traveling wasn’t just transport; it became an experience in itself. His skill and calm energy added so much charm to our time in Srinagar.

Overall, the hospitality at Sukoon Houseboat is world-class, delivered by a team that treats guests with genuine care and warmth. We couldn’t have asked for a better stay.
Highly, highly recommended.
reviews.author: prithviraj prasad

reviews.text: Sukoon boathouse is located opp to ghat 19 on dal lake. it is a quite side of dal lake and has a beautiful deck. it is a beautiful boat house. They need to do some up keep like toilets need exhaust, hot water comes after 15 mins of water being wasted to get lake warm water 5 mins after that to get hot water. the bathroom fixtures need changes, toiletries need to be provided as per occupancy, doors are a bit jammed and so are locks. food is good but service is a but lost and slow. the music performance of Santoor performer was the highlight of the stay. Behaviour of the owner on the last hour of my stay was the lowest point. The owner was questioning my booking at the end of our stay. I had booked a one night stay for 3 members with breakfast and lunch or dinner option for 21k. I booked one hour prior to our arrival to the property at 4ish pm. The owner or whoever was sitting at the boathouse reception was rude and was telling me that its not possible to get the deal at this cost. This was a rude behavior, Firstly he had no decency to first ask how was our stay at the property and second he was questioning my voucher of booking as if I got a free stay at his privileged property. I find this kind of entitled behavior very rude. It spoils the mood. The staff is far better behaved. Overall a good property and good food, good staff, avoid talking to the owner or who ever is managing the books. Keep your booking voucher handy, he has a tendency to be rude. The place has good view. Be ready to experience complete quite and peace. I would request the property to make the stay more welcoming as the cost is not low so hospitality needs to be the prime focus.
reviews.author: sabah

reviews.text:
We recently stayed at Sukoon Houseboat on Dal Lake for two nights, booked via Kashmir Express, with high expectations for a boutique luxury experience. The location is undeniably beautiful, and the houseboat has a charming, traditional aesthetic that reflects the region’s heritage. However, several aspects of the stay fell short of the level of service and comfort typically associated with luxury hospitality.

On arrival, our welcome high tea was delayed, leaving us waiting in the heat. Additionally, the tour operator began filming our family without prior consent for social media purposes, which felt intrusive and inappropriate during what was meant to be private time.

Our Shikara ride was scheduled too close to dusk, which limited visibility and photo opportunities. The ride was also repeatedly interrupted by floating vendors, which impacted the peaceful experience we were hoping for.

Service issues were unfortunately a recurring theme. Despite several reminders to the manager and staff on duty, room cleaning did not happen until late at night after we returned from a long day of sightseeing. The bathroom in our suite had maintenance issues, including a clogged bathtub and poor drainage. Due to the humidity, we had to request a fan for basic comfort.

Room service did not reflect the standard expected from a premium property, and attention to cleanliness was lacking. Drinking water was served in flasks that did not appear properly cleaned, and we discovered hair in our breakfast. This concern was raised in the presence of Mr. Jaseer, who witnessed the issue.

To add to our discomfort, we also encountered a large spider in the bathroom, which was unsettling, especially considering this was positioned as a luxury suite. While we understand that natural settings come with the occasional insect, we expected a higher standard of room upkeep and pest control at this level.

The property layout also had practical drawbacks. The lobby serves as the reception area, leading to frequent foot traffic and limited privacy. There were no recreational amenities—such as books, games, or a lounge area—for adults or children to enjoy during downtime. In-room features like lights, air conditioning, and curtains had to be operated individually, which felt outdated and inconvenient.

We had paid for an extra bed for our child and were upgraded to what was described as the “most luxurious suite.” While the gesture was appreciated, the suite itself felt basic compared to other high-end houseboats we’ve previously stayed at—particularly on Nigeen Lake.

Kashmir is truly breathtaking, and we believe it deserves a level of hospitality that complements its natural beauty. Sukoon Houseboat has the potential to offer a remarkable guest experience, but significant improvements in service quality, maintenance, hygiene, and guest privacy are needed to meet the expectations of discerning travellers.
reviews.author: Fiza k

reviews.text:
⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ An Unmatched, Dreamy Family Getaway!
​Our 3-night stay at Coco Shambhala this week with my wife and children was nothing short of a dreamy vacation. The entire property and our villa were an absolute paradise—tastefully designed, architectural masterpieces that are excellently maintained down to the finest detail.
​What truly elevates this place from a luxury resort to an unforgettable experience is its people. A huge, heartfelt thank you to Revan and Suhas, for their flawless coordination and attentive care. Moreover the entire staff deserves immense appreciation for providing service with the utmost sincerity and a constant, genuine smile on their faces.
​We have stayed at some of the finest properties across the country, but the hospitality at Coco Shambhala easily surpasses even the Taj group of hotels. The level of warmth, personalization, and effortless luxury here is unmatched.
​If you are looking for an extraordinary sanctuary for a family holiday, this is it. We cannot wait to return!
reviews.author: md afham

reviews.text: We have just spent 7 nights at Coco Shambhala and it was one of the most relaxing resort stays I have ever had.  A quit corner of India to recharge the batteries.  Staff were super friendly, the food was amazing, and a short walk to the beach when I could tear myself away from the villa's infinity pool with the amazing ocean views.  Will definitely be back when I am in India again.
reviews.author: Ian Frentz

reviews.text: So we had Coco Shamballa in our bucket list for quite some time.. and we had seen numerous images of the Villas on instagram and even more images of Delectable Food ! .. so we thought we knew what to expect...... but what we got back from this break.. was beyond expectations... Yes the Villa Is beautiful.. but you truly have to experience its tropical vibe .. we have spent hours just sitting in the living area staring at the endless darkness in the night ... and yes the food does look good in the images.. but what we got was every meal curated to immaculate perfection.. this was beyond what we expected.. and we have truly never had such lip smacking meals.... but beyond the meals and the villa its the humble hardworking people who make coco what it is..... from arranging cycles for us to ride during our visit.. to arranging anti motion sickness medicines for our scuba diving trip.. the team made sure that we were looked for.. i would highly recommend you to hire cycles and meander across the endless roads and discover temples and beaches. and do go for the nivati fort trek... the nivati beach at the end of the trek is one of the most beaufiful beaches we have visited.. our special mentions to Lumen for fast and efficient bookings , Suhas for taking care of our culinary needs and every whim and fancy... Suhas is an amazing hospitality professional.. with great stories to share.. . a big thank you to Vaibhav our naturalist for patiently accompanying us on our hikes and treks and Amol, dinesh and many more in the service staff who did their best to ensure a comfortable stay.. we will surely be back!!
reviews.author: Conifer Handmades

reviews.text:
What an experience! We booked all of the 4 villas which really enhances the experience, in my opinion. The villas are incredibly designed, maintained & are very pet/kids friendly.
The food is incredible & do not miss the BBQ! A true pleasure & we’ll be back very soon :)
reviews.author: Stuti Desai

reviews.text:
Where do I start in describing a most wonderful gathering in Coco's clifftop villas above Bhogwe beach?
A group of 20 of friends came together to celebrate my 60th birthday , travelling from the US, the UK, Denmark, Sweden, Austria, Italy, Tanzania, Ethiopia, India, New Zealand and Australia. I'd booked all 4 villas for three nights and right from the get-go the management and staff at Coco did everything they could to make our gathering an event of a lifetime.

We were awed by the beauty of the Coco Shambhala concept and the panache with which the vision has been executed - a huge hongera sana (congrats in Kiswahli 🙂) to the owner of Coco! We were treated like family - thank you, Revan and all the housekeeping staff for your kindnesses and smiling attention to our various (sometimes troublesome, no doubt) needs.  We dined like kings - Suhas and his sous chefs are such inventive geniuses (pretty much all of us are foodies and the consensus was "I couldn't fault a thing") and we are now testing the recipes they kindly shared on our friends and family (the karripata lemonade - oof!). Rachel handled the reservation process with patience, a sense of humour and sensitive advice - thank you, again.
We were treated to a magnificent bonfire and fireworks display that eclipsed this year's alignment of Mars, Jupiter and Saturn.
We hiked the paths overlooking what must surely be one of the most pristine beaches in India.
We had an unforgettable 'formal' birthday dinner by candlelight and starlight under the Coco cashew tree.
We putt-putted in small boats to the neighbouring Nivati Cove, to explore a new beach where Suhas and his team had a pop-up street-food stall - such a perfect idea!
We lounged by the clear aqua infinity pools in our villas, chatting and reminiscing about our long friendships, hushed by the sunset vistas, and wishing we could stay forever at Coco.
What can I say? If you're thinking of visiting Coco - stop! Don't dream, just go!
Thank you, thank you, thank you all at Coco. I hope to see you again soon.
With love and gratitude,
Criana Connal
reviews.author: Criana Connal

reviews.text:
If you are looking for an uncommercialized, peaceful basecamp in Kerala, Marari Sea Scape Villa is an absolute standout.
Location & Vibe:
The property is situated barely 100 meters from the white sands of Marari Beach—literally a one-minute walk. For early morning coastal drone flights and golden-hour photography, the accessibility is unbeatable. The villa itself is surrounded by lush greenery, complete with mango trees that drop fresh fruit, giving it a very authentic, relaxed Kerala homestay feel.
Hospitality & Comfort:
The host, Binoy, and his family are incredibly warm and accommodating. The rooms are simple but meticulously clean, well-maintained, and equipped with reliable AC and hot water. Traveling with an infant and delicate camera equipment means I need a space that is both secure and comfortable, and this villa provided exactly that kind of stress-free environment.
Food & Amenities:
They serve delicious home-cooked meals, though you do need to let them know your preferences (veg or non-veg) a bit in advance so they can prepare. The serene atmosphere makes it a great place to unwind after a long day of travel or location scouting.
Highly recommended for anyone looking for exceptional value, genuine hospitality, and instant beach access without the crowds of large resorts.
#MarariBeach #KeralaHomestay #HiddenGems #TravelKerala
reviews.author: Rajan Kapoor

reviews.text: Walkable distance from beach. Very pleasant environment. Friendly host. Had subtle in-house breakfast. Very clean rooms. Great experience. Worth the money
reviews.author: Dhanya T

reviews.text: Such a worst host service ever i had till date, not even a basic things were provided, shocking was there wasn't even blanket not even a bottle of water at-least. I didn’t even able to sleep well because the property is undergoing roofing works done, if they could have mentioned it before i checked in i could definitely took their neighborhood homestay. Because I clearly told them before I checked in , that I wanted to go to my office which is in near by , in between in the night because there were deep cleaners were working until 3’o clock in the morning, so I couldn’t get continues sleep in the night as well, So when I came to sleep as soon as I finished my breakfast their workers started drilling etc in the top of my room. When I complained about the disturbance which I was facing while sleep they asked me to check out 10 in the morning without any guilty. Wt a bad service i got. And I was the only guest were there in their whole property they had for a night , still they were not at all welcoming with even a good smile at-least, such a bad experience i had and very much disappointed.

reviews.text: We ended up in this structure not by choice but it was a very pleasant surprise. Simple, super clean very nice family and we discovered an excellent meal all made by the family. To recommend
reviews.author: Alfredo De Feo

reviews.text:
⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ Wonderful Stay at Marari Sea Skape Villa!

Our stay at Marari Sea Skape Villa was amazing. The hosts were warm and welcoming, the rooms were clean and comfortable, and the atmosphere was peaceful and relaxing. The food was delicious and freshly prepared, making the experience even better.

Highly recommended for anyone looking for a calm and memorable getaway. Would love to visit again.
reviews.author: ADWIN VK

reviews.text: Nice and spacious 2bhk apartment. All basic amenities available . Not too much clutter . Silent residential location overlooking a river . Outdoor surroundings are not in hosts capacity so not to upscale . Once inside it’s a different feeling .
reviews.author: Mahesh Kumar

reviews.text:
Worst Experience Ever – Misleading, Unhygienic & Unprofessional!

We booked this property in Siolim through Airbnb specifically because of the lake view they advertised. That was the highlight of the listing and the reason we paid for it. But when we arrived, we were given a completely different property on the same floor with no lake view at all. There were two properties on that floor, and the one we were given was the exact opposite of what we booked. This is nothing less than a scam.

The property was supposed to accommodate 4 people, but it was not even set up properly for 2. Here’s what we actually faced:
•	Dental Kit: Completely unhygienic — it had fungus on it.
•	Kitchen: The induction didn’t work. Utensils were barely available, not enough for 4 people, and what was there was in poor condition.
•	Sofa: Covers were dumped aside. Were we supposed to put them on ourselves after paying for a stay?
•	Mosquitoes: The place was full of them, making it impossible to sit inside peacefully.
•	Speakers: The listing mentioned speakers, but there were none. Another false promise.

And the management made everything worse. First, the caretaker on-site told us that what we paid was just a “base price” — an absurd excuse. Airbnb doesn’t work like that. The platform shows guests the final price upfront, there is no such thing as base versus full price. Clearly, this was just an attempt to cover up their mismanagement.

When we contacted the manager, she was completely unhelpful. She refused to acknowledge any of the issues, flatly denied a refund, and wasn’t ready to take responsibility at all. Every single thing we asked for, she replied with “impossible.” We even requested the contact details of the owner, and she refused to provide that as well. The attitude was rude, dismissive, and unacceptable.

This entire experience shows how little they care about guests. The property is unhygienic, misleading, and poorly maintained. The staff and manager are unprofessional and unwilling to resolve even the simplest issues.

If Airbnb values its credibility, properties like this should be taken down immediately.

To anyone considering this place: Do not book it. There are far better stays in Siolim where you’ll actually get what you’re promised and be treated with basic respect. This one is a waste of money and will leave you frustrated.
reviews.author: Samiksha Sawant

reviews.text: Great place for short stays. Good ambience with spacious rooms and clean bathrooms. The balcony has good views of a farm field in the back and river in front. The kitchen had all necessary utensils, induction cooker, fridge etc. The staff were really friendly and helpful.
reviews.author: Delbin Thomas

reviews.text:
**Terrible Experience – Don’t Stay Here!**

I reached at this villa in Goa with my family, and it was an absolute nightmare from start.. To begin with, the air conditioning was not working in three bedrooms and the living area, despite the intense heat. We couldn’t get in touch with the owner even once to address the issue – every call went unanswered.

The hand faucet pipe in one of the bathrooms broke while we were using it, and to make matters worse, the caretaker said they'll leave the property at 6 pm every day, so after that, we were completely unattended. The location is also far from ideal, and the photos on the website are heavily edited to look much better than it is in reality.

My elderly parents, aunt, and uncle all fell ill because of the unbearable heat and lack of proper amenities(broken washroom). Even after we left, the owner didn’t respond to our calls, let alone offer any form of compensation or refund. We paid 12,000 in advance and received nothing close to what was promised.

*Save yourself the trouble and avoid this place – it was the worst experience we’ve ever had*.
reviews.author: Garima

reviews.text: Deceived by the edited  pictures of property.  Management also turn deaf ear when we told them it's completely opposite to our expectation. We had  paid full advance for booking  and still decided not to stay even for single day there because it over looks heaps of garbage and also locals who stay just below this house were drying their underwears just at the entrance of the property . The property approach is appalling. We did tell the management that we didn't like the property, but they never replied and never refunded even a dime of our money and usurped the whole amount . If you want to ruin your and your family holiday mood by spending on this property then be their guest.
reviews.author: bhumit salgiya

reviews.text:
On 10th December 2025 i reached the RIVER HOUSE property! Before reaching there i was a bit anxious as to how it would be. But when saw the premises i was mesmerized! One the best accomodations in which i have stsyed so far. Awesome, beautiful, superb and what not. I am falling short of the adjectives.
To add to it excellent service, very yummy food for dinner as well as the breakfast. Excellent is the word applicable.
I was there barrly for 21 hours but till now regretting that i got very short time there. Would love to visit again.
All credit to the owners of the property and the dedicated staff.
Rooms: 5/5  |  Service:
reviews.author: sanjeev modak

reviews.text: A stunningly beautiful, enormous house with only five guest rooms all very spaced out. A glorious walkway through lush gardens full of flowers and spice trees to a  refreshing pool and a covered, but glorious open air restaurant that overlooks the river. Lovely, comfortable, spacious and simply appointed room. We were lucky enough to be hosted by the delightful Dr. Simon who along with his lovely wife Molly, created this resort (and the Windemere Estate, also outstanding).  The food was fabulous, made from the best of the day, every meal was a feast with the wonderful chef very happy to cater for different diets/tastes.  Standout staff   - though all were charming - were Prakash and Tony. We also had a few great early morning yoga sessions with Rosilyn who introduced us to a very different way of practice which we really enjoyed. Highly recommend!
reviews.author: Eileen Wada Willett

reviews.text:
A beautiful hotel, actually it’s not a hotel but a home and I can’t think of enough superlatives. Imagine your idea of paradise with a lovely pool, the sound of birds and lovely tropical gardens and this is it. The food was fantastic and Tony who was our waiter is brilliant 🤩. John, who is the manager/owner could not do enough for us. Calm, knowledgeable and very helpful and kind. We had 2 yoga sessions with Rosilyn who runs a local yoga centre. She was amazing - we’re converted !
Must also take the morning or/and afternoon bird walks with Dinesh (apologies if the spelling is incorrect). He’s such a nice man, so engaging and a brilliant bird spotter!
reviews.author: Jo Hatton

reviews.text:
We stayed for one night at Windermere River House and had the most magical time. Their service is second to none. The staff could not have done more for us, and the room was beautiful.

The estate has so many birds, butterflies and flowers. We ate lunch, dinner and breakfast at Windermere River House and were impressed with each meal.

If you are considering visiting this beautiful area, this is the place to stay.
reviews.author: Jess Timperley

reviews.text: I throughly enjoyed my yoga class at Windermere River House with Rosalyn. I've been practicing yoga in the US for over 40 years. I've never had in depth discussion on mudras and proper breathing and how the two promote therapeutic healing. Rosalyn is a jewel to be experienced.
reviews.author: Don Hawley

kolukkumalai-tea-bungalow.generativeSummary: Tea estate at 7,000+ feet featuring scenic views, adventure activities and a 100-year-old factory.

reviews.text: A vacation at Kolukkumalai Tea Estate feels like stepping into a quiet dream perched above the clouds, where time slows down and nature takes the lead. As the world’s highest tea plantation, it offers sweeping views of mist-covered valleys, crisp mountain air, and endless stretches of emerald tea bushes that glow softly at sunrise. Mornings begin with the gentle hum of the wind and a cup of freshly brewed tea, rich and aromatic, made from leaves plucked just steps away. Wandering through the estate, watching traditional tea-making methods, and soaking in the serene silence create a deep sense of calm. It’s a place where simplicity becomes luxurious, and every moment feels refreshingly pure and unforgettable. 🍃☕
reviews.author: Samuel Joseph Raj

reviews.text: Fantastic sunrise views, fresh cold air, and an unforgettable atmosphere. The peaceful tea plantations and mountain scenery felt completely different after Bangalore’s heat and routine. Early morning at Kolukkumalai has a calmness that pictures honestly cannot fully capture. The jeep ride was rough but absolutely worth the experience. Perfect place if you want nature, silence, and a refreshing break from city life.
reviews.author: B K

reviews.text: Must visit place. World's highest organic tea estate. Beautiful places. We can see all procedures in tea factory. From leaf to tea powder. We can buy organic tea powder. If we intrested stay here we can. Many travellers and tourists visiting here for trekking. We can see very beautiful sunrise here. Here have a beautiful view point. Strongly recommends.
reviews.author: Travel Notes by Prasad

reviews.text: Superb location... Maybe not suitable for aged people.. Really rough offroad drive.. Perfect for thrill seekers.. Will visit again definitely.. Bcoz the view was worth it at the top.. Heavenly
reviews.author: Sreeji P.S

reviews.text: Excellent sunrise, drizzle, chillness, smal hike , a roller coaster ride
reviews.author: MD N

tranquil-resort-wayanad.editorialSummary: Laid-back rooms & treehouses on a tranquil coffee/spice plantation offering free breakfast & dinner.

reviews.text:
An exceptional and truly memorable stay. The amazing treehouse accommodation was beautifully designed and offered a sense of privacy, peace, and tranquillity. The setting provided an ideal environment to fully unwind, with really enjoyable walks through the coffee plantation and stunning views of the surrounding nature. The food was outstanding throughout, and the hospitality was warm, and highly personalised.

A personal thank you to Ajay, Nisha, and all of the amazing staff for making the experience so special. A standout stay that I would highly recommend to anyone seeking a high-quality, tranquil retreat.
reviews.author: Paul Busa

reviews.text: We had a fantastic 3 days at Tranquil Resort. An exceptional and beautiful place and a real chance to escape and relax. There are so many well marked walking trails through the coffee plantations and above the house - stunning views everywhere. We had a great early morning walk  around the estate with a bird expert and saw so many colourful birds and also the giant Malabar squirrels. There's a lovely pool and a games room. Every meal was a delicious feast. Ajay and Nisha are wonderful hosts - so warm and friendly. All the staff are lovely and nothing is too much trouble.  Highly recommended!!
reviews.author: Lucinda Green

reviews.text: We chose Tranquil to celebrate our 30th wedding anniversary & were not disappointed. Ajay & Nisha gave us the warmest welcome & we were spoilt by the lovely staff. The scrumptious buffet was walked off on the many trails around the estate. A true haven of peace & tranquility to enjoy all nature has to offer. We didn't want to leave this little piece of paradise. Thank you Ajay & Nisha for such a wonderful memorable holiday.
reviews.author: james tweed

reviews.text: It is a very nice huge plantation spread across 400 acres. Has many numbers of walking trails to suit most campers !! Food is excellent for both Veg and nonveg!!!
reviews.author: Gopal Sunder rao

reviews.text:
I wanted to share my wonderful experience at Tranquil, a homestay on the Kapamudi Coffee Estate in Wayanad. As someone who frequently visits homestays across the Western Ghats due to my love for nature and mountains, Tranquil truly stands out, largely due to the exceptional hospitality of its hosts, Nisha and Ajay.

Their personal touch makes a significant difference. From a refreshing cold towel upon arrival to their genuine interest in every meal, their commitment to guest satisfaction is evident. The rooms are spacious and impeccably clean, with crisp white linen and perfectly ironed towels. The overall cleanliness, even in the expansive 400-acre property, was impressive—I encountered virtually no bugs or insects, which was a pleasant surprise.

The staff also deserves high praise; they are humble, kind, and attentive, a testament to Nisha's excellent training. The food was cooked to perfection, contributing greatly to the overall delightful experience.

I highly recommend Tranquil to anyone seeking a top-tier homestay experience.
reviews.author: Joel Fernandez

reviews.text:
I visited Honey Valley a few years ago but I'm posting my review only now. The property is EXCELLENT. Mr Suresh is a wonderful host.

The rooms define simplicity and has all the basics one requires. Delicious food is served at fixed timings as a buffet and the rates are reasonable.

The property can be reached only a 4x4 and they have car parking at the entrance (bottom) of the property. There are 2 levels - level 1 is Chingaara which is the more premium offering and level 2 at a higher elevation is Honey Valley.

There's a brilliant private waterfall situated on the property too. Located close to Tadiyandamol peak too, a must-do trek whilst in Coorg.

Wake up early in the morning and you're greeted by mesmerizing greenery covered in mist which slowly clears out showcasing the unbeatable views right from your room window. Honey Valley layout is very well laid out with offerings for varied budgets and even solo backpacker rooms.

Such a beautiful place this! I often reminisce my time here - away from all the chaotic city life.

Can't wait to be back here again. My best regards to Mr Suresh and his wife :)
reviews.author: Eve Maria

reviews.text:
Perfect for a Digital detox... The stay was amazing with very cozy and clean rooms built in traditional style cottages, surrounded by lush green forest and coffee plantation this is a perfect spot to blend in with nature and get away from everything that is digital. The owner Mr. Suresh and his wife are amazing people. The staff very friendly. Not a single moment her felt like out of place.

Tourists beware! No 5G or even 4G network here. Calls on Airtel barely make it out, but I'd say that's a selling point.
reviews.author: Atul Bhat

reviews.text: We stayed three nights at Honey Valley and we loved it. The nature is so beautiful at this remote spot and the owners and the whole team were lovely and super helpful. We spent our time just looking and listening into the forest, walking around, reading and eating and it was very relaxing. And the food for breakfast, lunch and dinner was amazing and we will miss it very much back in Germany. We recommend Honey Valley to anyone who wants to simply enjoy nature and food.
reviews.author: A G

reviews.text:
Visited with my wife, sister, and brother-in-law, and we all had a wonderful time. This resort is perfect if you want to escape city life and be close to nature. Surrounded by lush greenery, you wake up to birds chirping, and it’s a paradise for birdwatching and herping. I spotted multiple frog species, two cat snakes, and even a Malabar Pit Viper!

Special thanks to the owner, Suresh Sir, whose knowledge of nature and warm hospitality, along with the friendly staff, made our stay truly memorable.  The private waterfall is another highlight.

Highly recommend this place for nature lovers seeking peace and serenity. Not meant for parties or nightlife—come here only if you genuinely love nature.
reviews.author: Arun Prakash

reviews.text:
We had a very unpleasant experience at this homestay due to the attitude of the staff. They were uncooperative and showed no consideration for guests’ basic needs. When we requested milk to be boiled for our infant, they flatly refused saying their kitchen is always busy preparing next meals. They also denied making watermelon juice for the baby, even though we clearly mentioned that it could be made from our own buffet share and later we provided the fruit for the juice. Such rigid and insensitive behavior is unacceptable in the hospitality business. The staff seriously lack customer service orientation and make the stay uncomfortable for families. Definitely not a baby-friendly place.

Families with infants definitely have to reconsider their decision before booking this place.

Food is also below average.

Although food is not included, they will bill for all the head counts irrespective of whether a person will have food in their restaurant or not.

Location of the stay is beyond words to describe. Mesmerising views and nature.

Price of the stay is affordable but with little higher budget we wouldn’t have faced these issues. We had booked this stay  in the last minute during Dussehra festival so we didn’t have much choices then. But I wouldn’t recommend this place to anyone.
reviews.author: Rajesh R

shreyas-retreat-bengaluru.generativeSummary: Resort providing luxurious rooms for short stays, plus yoga and spa facilities.
shreyas-retreat-bengaluru.editorialSummary: Airy cottages in a serene resort offering yoga classes & a spa, plus free meals & drinks.

reviews.text:
Excellent experience worth a visit for ayurvedic treatments
Staff is beyond 5 star service
food is veg but amazing
Plan for at-least a week long stay
reviews.author: Aditya Santpur

reviews.text: Lovely food, ambience and. The Vegans in our group were also very happy. The choices were limited for them but food was delicious. You have choice of selection and way of cooking. Lovely and efficient service. We thoroughly enjoyed it
reviews.author: Bhuvan Gasyal

reviews.text:
I stayed at Shreyas over the recent Xmas holidays. Compared to my stay 4 years ago, my experience was mixed.
Pros:  The Ayurvedic oil massage was amazing.  The spa treatments are relaxing. I especially liked the steam room.  For the classes, I enjoyed the meditation instruction.

Cons:
My dining experience was subpar. There were few options for a light diet, as I had requested. I ended up just having toast and tea, and no protein options.  I missed the group dining set up from the prior stay, where I got to meet people from around the world. This time, the table set up felt isolating, and meals were quiet and the environment felt somewhat depressing. I learned that the retreat is focusing on treatment and discouraging socializing.

I enjoyed the cottage experience the last time, but there were upkeep issues this time. On my first day, Black ants were crawling on the toilet in my room. It required multiple follow-ups to get a change to a new room.

The yoga classes were okay. On my last day, I learned from another visitor, that there were more advanced yoga classes.

Overall, this retreat fits boutique, but no longer luxurious, in my experience.
reviews.author: Nina Agrawal MD

reviews.text:
Very pleasent and luxurious place.
We experience the excellent hospitality here.
Highly recommend for people of physical and mental illness & for busy professionals to take break here.
Yoga sessions here are amazing.
Very healthy and tasty food. Booked for 5 days but stayed for 10 days the place is magical
reviews.author: Rohit goyal

reviews.text:
Lovely stay with awesome service.
We can experience healthy organic food, yoga etc and a very good place to relax. You can select this without any doubt if you need to stay relax and calmly .... Loved it... :) :) :)
reviews.author: Arun Nair

siolim-house-goa.editorialSummary: Polished hotel in a 1675 Portuguese manor with elegant rooms & suites, plus fine dining.

reviews.text: Shivali and her lovely polite and ever vigilant team, made us feel so welcomed and relaxed in this shabby chic style boutique 10 roomed hotel, with a nice breakfast and snack and salad menu. Perfect for a short restful break on your tour of Goa, the swimming pool is a real highlight as it is quite large for such a small venue. Laid back, but very close to excellent array of high end restaurants, fabulous beaches and about 90 minutes drive to the airport. Overall an overly place to stay!
reviews.author: Mary Keane-Dawson

reviews.text: The building is over 300 years old. The rooms are large and have antique furniture and the entire vibe is around the age of the building, with the quite spaces adding to the tranquility. The have a beautiful courtyard with a well, which is an experience in itself. They also have a small pool which is good for a soak but not large enough for a workout. They have some seating around the pool and a gandola as well. There is a small massage room with a single bed. It's a must stay if you're a history lover, else the location can be better
reviews.author: Hannan Khan

reviews.text:
Siolim House is tucked away in the most peaceful setting, it offers a quiet, almost restorative escape from the usual rush of life. The space itself is beautiful, thoughtfully designed, full of charm, and effortlessly serene. The rooms are beautifully designed and feel like home away from home.

The owners (Varun, Lucy and his mother) and staff are incredibly warm, attentive, and genuinely kind, making you feel at home from the moment you arrive. Special shout out to Shivali, Marqer, Dexter, Kevin, Devender and Mojri who made my stay so comfortable.

It’s the perfect place to slow down, reset, and just be. I left feeling lighter, more rested, and already looking forward to coming back. Highly, highly recommend.
reviews.author: Shriya Raychaudhuri

reviews.text:
What a wonderful place to stay at! Beautiful architecture - a gorgeous property, super rustic and maintained amazingly.

The rooms are really warmly done up, full of character and yet luxurious and not imposing.
The breakfast is delicious, they have homemade bread, jam and other bakes, fresh juices and a special mention about their tea time - a variety of teas and special homemade bakes which were  honestly to die for! Soft buttery tea cakes and cookies. Yum yum yum.

It’s super quaint, with a lovely pool, and lots of places to lounge. Definitely recommend staying here.
reviews.author: Johann Vikram Singh

reviews.text:
This was our 3rd visit. Siolim House is located in the bustling town of Siolim - some 15/20 minutes by car from the nearest beach and although the village has developed over recent years it still retains a local feel. Siolim House is a beautifully restored property with an old world feel. It’s quirky, quaint and full of character. The upstairs bedrooms in particular are lovely; high ceilings, big bathrooms, four poster beds with mosquito nets, ceiling fans and a/c. The staff are welcoming and very helpful, many have been there a long time.  It’s within walking distance of a very good restaurant and a short taxi ride to a wider choice .
We really enjoyed our stay there and will without doubt return.
reviews.author: Stephanie

reviews.text: One of the oldest tea estate . Very quite . Despite of being  an off season, the factory manager showed me the whole place , explained the process of making , sorting , packing the tea leaves , he showed me around and we really had a wonderful chat . Place and vibe felt really authentic .
reviews.author: suchetana mondal

reviews.text: Various types of Organic tea available at factory outlet, pupils can visit and experience the processing of tea leaves to packaging of tea in the factory. Mr. Laltu babu at the outlet is much knowledgeable about tea processing and preparation. The factory closed on Sunday and other holidays. Factory visit cost Rs 100 per person.
reviews.author: Atanu Das

reviews.text: This is a very famous tea estate near Kurseong known for it's renowned flavours of Darjeeling tea. As it's a known brand, the tea boxes could be quite expensive. However the quality is one of the finest. If it's a harvest season, you can see the tea processing in the factory too at a price. There is also a boutique shop where you can buy tea packets to carry for your home. Overall a great experience.
reviews.author: Kallol Das

reviews.text: I didn’t stay here, just went to the tea demonstration as I was walking around the gardens. I went to this tea estate as it’s fair trade and treats the workers better.   They also offer many delicious herbal teas.  This village is stunning, the clean air and quiet is welcome after the crowds in Darjeeling.
reviews.author: Susan Fernandez-Nemes

reviews.text:
It's perfect for buying excellent tea
They sell in wholesale rate
reviews.author: Ipsita Mukherjee
